name: Comet Six
explanation: A six-voice analog-style polyphonic synthesizer in the lineage of the early-80s multimode flagships. Each voice runs two oscillators — a band-limited saw and pulse — with oscillator cross-modulation and a touch of hard-sync grit for metallic edge, feeding a resonant state-variable filter whose MODE continuously morphs between low-pass, band-pass and high-pass for hollow, bright and nasal poly tones a plain low-pass cannot reach. A dedicated filter envelope and an amplitude AR shape every voice, so chords ring independently. Controls: Cutoff, Resonance, Filter Mode, Env Amount, Cross-Mod, Attack, Release and Level.
